objective: Validate the beginner-friendly mobile web, PWA, and Capacitor app experience before external users or store reviewers use the product.

officialReferences.name: Apple App Store Connect screenshot specifications
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Store screenshots must match accepted device families and avoid misleading preview assets.

officialReferences.name: Google Play preview asset guidelines
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Google Play screenshots and preview graphics must show real app value and meet size and copy rules.

officialReferences.name: web.dev PWA installation guidance
officialReferences.whyItMatters: PWA QA must confirm installability, standalone launch, manifest identity, and platform install prompts.

officialReferences.name: Capacitor documentation
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Native iOS and Android packages must be validated through Capacitor sync, platform builds, and real-device behavior.

officialReferences.name: Google Play Data safety
officialReferences.whyItMatters: Mobile QA must keep privacy, account deletion, and data access paths visible to users and reviewers.

deviceMatrix.label: Android phone packaged app
deviceMatrix.blocker: ANDROID_HOME or ANDROID_SDK_ROOT and JDK 17+ are required before signed Android device validation.

deviceMatrix.label: iPhone packaged app
deviceMatrix.blocker: iOS build, signing, and TestFlight/device QA must run on macOS with Xcode.

testSuites.title: Beginner home and large button launcher

cases.title: Video mode starts with obvious beginner actions
steps: Open the app on a 390px phone viewport.
Confirm the Video segment is selected by default.
Scan the large two-column action cards without reading any help text.
cases.expectedResult: A first-time user can identify the main video actions from icon, label, and badge without using the desktop navigation.

cases.title: Image/text mode switches in place
steps: Tap the Image/Text segment.
Confirm the card set changes without page reload.
Check the prompt-package and product-image entries are visible.
cases.expectedResult: The user sees a simpler image/text task set with large touch targets and no layout jump.

cases.title: Notification panel opens as a compact mobile card
steps: Open the app on a 390px phone viewport.
Tap the notification bell in the top bar.
Confirm the panel stays inside the screen and does not become a right-side strip.
cases.expectedResult: Task reminders open as a compact card below the top bar, with internal scrolling and no overlap with the bottom navigation.

testSuites.title: Guided entry routes to the correct creation step

cases.title: Prompt Package card opens the prompt step
steps: Open home.
Tap Image/Text.
Tap Prompt Package.
cases.expectedResult: The create page opens directly on the prompt-package step instead of dropping the user on a generic dashboard.

cases.title: Product Image card opens the image upload step
steps: Open home.
Tap Image/Text.
Tap Upload Product Image.
cases.expectedResult: The create page focuses on the product-image input so a beginner does not need to understand the full workflow first.

cases.title: Beginner operation cards show more actions without becoming tiny
steps: Open the app on 390px and 430px phone viewports.
Review the beginner launcher in the default Video mode.
Confirm action cards, circular icons, labels, and badges fit without large blank areas.
cases.expectedResult: The first screen exposes more useful actions than the earlier oversized version while keeping operation cards finger-friendly.

cases.title: Create and overview controls stay compact on phones
steps: Open production overview and create on a 390px phone viewport.
Check status cards, create tabs, input step cards, and bottom navigation.
Confirm the bottom navigation does not consume excessive vertical space.
cases.expectedResult: Overview and create pages keep enough content visible for a beginner to continue without excessive scrolling.

testSuites.title: Login, session, and review account path

cases.title: Dedicated review user can sign in
steps: Open the public HTTPS app.
Sign in with the dedicated review account.
Close and reopen the app.
cases.expectedResult: The reviewer remains signed in or is returned to a clear login state without losing the intended task path.

cases.title: Production session uses hardened cookie and origin settings
steps: Deploy to the final HTTPS origin.
Run the production smoke test.
Confirm cookie and CORS settings match the release runbook.
cases.expectedResult: Authenticated mobile sessions work only for allowed production origins and do not rely on localhost.

cases.title: User can add product image material
steps: Open Create from the beginner launcher.
Use the file picker to add a small product image.
Confirm the file appears in the form or material list.
cases.expectedResult: The user can add material without desktop-only controls, hidden buttons, or text overlap.

cases.title: User can submit a dry-run generation task
steps: Fill the minimum product fields.
Generate or paste a prompt package.
Submit a dry-run video task.
cases.expectedResult: The task is created, visible in the task list, and does not require a real video provider during review dry-run.

cases.title: Assets are usable as mobile cards
steps: Open Assets from the bottom navigation.
Scan product, prompt, task, and result cards.
Open a visible asset detail or preview action.
cases.expectedResult: The mobile view does not expose desktop tables as the primary surface and cards remain readable.

cases.title: Video stitch flow is reachable on mobile
steps: Open the stitch page from the beginner launcher or route.
Check the mobile stitch video card list.
Confirm table-only interactions are not required.
cases.expectedResult: The mobile stitch path can be reviewed without horizontal table scrolling.

testSuites.title: Settings, legal pages, and data rights

cases.title: Legal and support links are reachable
steps: Open Settings.
Tap privacy, terms, AI disclosure, support, and account deletion links.
Return to the app after each link.
cases.expectedResult: Users and reviewers can find legal and support information from the mobile app without hidden desktop navigation.

cases.title: Data export and deletion request paths are visible
steps: Open Settings.
Start data export.
Start account deletion/data rights request.
cases.expectedResult: The app presents a clear request state and does not ask users to email support as the only path.

cases.title: Pilot user can submit feedback from mobile settings
steps: Open Settings on a phone viewport.
Find the trial feedback card below the app readiness center.
Submit a short issue with category and severity.
cases.expectedResult: The app records the feedback and returns a visible status without asking the user to upload private screenshots or logs.

cases.title: Android browser can install the PWA
steps: Open the public HTTPS app in Chrome on Android.
Wait for the install prompt or open browser install menu.
Install and launch from the home screen.
cases.expectedResult: The app launches in standalone mode with the correct icon, name, start URL, and bottom navigation.

cases.title: iOS user receives manual install guidance
steps: Open the public HTTPS app in Safari on iPhone.
Check the app install guidance.
Add to Home Screen and launch.
cases.expectedResult: The user sees a clear iOS install path and the app respects safe areas after launch.

cases.title: Android package builds and installs
steps: Install Android SDK and JDK 17 or newer.
Run Capacitor sync.
Build and install the app from Android Studio.
cases.expectedResult: The app installs on a real Android phone or emulator without cleartext traffic, debug WebView, or excessive permissions.

cases.title: Android hardware and WebView behavior is acceptable
steps: Launch the installed app.
Use back button, file picker, legal links, and settings.
Rotate once and return to portrait.
cases.expectedResult: Back navigation, file picker, external links, and safe-area spacing behave like a normal Android app.

cases.title: iOS package builds and installs
steps: Open the iOS project on macOS with Xcode.
Configure signing.
Build and install on iPhone or a store-accurate simulator.
cases.expectedResult: The app launches without broad ATS exceptions and shows the correct display name and launch screen.

cases.title: iOS safe area, file picker, and external links are acceptable
steps: Launch the installed app on iPhone.
Use home, create, assets, settings, and legal links.
Test file picker and return from external pages.
cases.expectedResult: No content is hidden by the notch, home indicator, browser chrome, or keyboard; file and link flows return cleanly.

cases.title: Web first-pass screenshots cover the core mobile story
steps: Review the mobile-web-390, mobile-web-430, and tablet-web-768 folders.
Check home, image mode, guided prompt entry, create, assets, settings, and stitch.
Confirm no TODO URL, local path, secret, or private data is visible.
cases.expectedResult: The web screenshot set can guide final native screenshot capture and copy review.

cases.title: Native store screenshot sets are captured after device builds
steps: Build iOS and Android native packages.
Capture screenshots for required App Store and Google Play device families.
Check copy, privacy, and reviewer-safe data.
cases.expectedResult: Store-ready screenshots match the native app, accepted dimensions, final locale copy, and privacy requirements.

testSuites.title: Touch targets, readability, and safe areas

cases.title: Primary mobile controls are finger-friendly
steps: Review home cards, segmented control, bottom navigation, and create tabs.
Tap each primary control with one hand.
Check that labels and badges do not resize or overlap.
cases.expectedResult: Buttons feel deliberately large for beginners and stay stable across 390px and 430px phones.

cases.title: Bottom navigation, keyboard, and safe areas do not hide content
steps: Open create and settings on a phone viewport.
Focus text fields and scroll to bottom content.
Check bottom navigation, keyboard, and safe-area spacing.
cases.expectedResult: Important actions remain reachable above the home indicator, browser UI, or virtual keyboard.

releaseDecisionRules: No P0 web/mobile-web case can be unknown before an external pilot.
No native app store submission can proceed while android-native, ios-native, or native-store-screenshots remains blocked.
A blocked manual case is acceptable for internal planning only when it names the exact toolchain, domain, review account, or native-build dependency.
Screenshots used for stores must come from the final build and must not show local paths, TODO URLs, secrets, private product data, or unreleased legal wording.
Every release-blocking QA issue must be copied into store/release-blockers-register.json with owner, unblock action, and verification command.
